mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean during rate cut periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering negative equity returns as recession fears dominate pricing.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with an information coefficient above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts yield negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side bottlenecks and manufacturing weakness, causing immediate negative market repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when industrial production falls below its trend, or the signal shows zero predictive power during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPIAUCSL readings above 5.0% trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation observations, or the signal's rank correlation with returns falls below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10), or positive payroll surprises consistently precede negative market moves over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and rising retail sales indicates inventory overhang and unsustainable consumption, prompting immediate negative market repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence yields positive 5-day returns in > 65% of cases, or the interaction effect fails to achieve statistical significance in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, increases in industrial production signal successful capacity utilization and earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production gains during recovery phases produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the mechanism loses predictive power outside the specified regime.
